script_content: [uncomfortable pause] That's... a lot to share with a stranger. [carefully measured] Perhaps you should consider a different career path. [quickly] Goodbye now, and please don't call again.
[dispassionately] The mark should exit the building in twelve minutes [suddenly booming] OH MY GOD that puppet bit! [voice tightening with suppressed anger] Stop making me laugh, I have a reputation.

[in feminine voice] Sophie's made a tactical error trying to navigate through the balloon forest, [laugh heartily] she's down, she's definitely down, [tone turning distant, as if distracted] wait, is that my mother-in-law filming this?

[slightly curious] Seventy percent? [back to monotone] That's either desperation or your product is overpriced to begin with. [Snorts] Neither option really inspires confidence in your company, does it?
[irritated] You've been stalking our tech stack? [sarcastic tone] How wonderfully invasive of you. [trailing off] Look, even if I wanted to meet, which I don't, I'd need board approval first.
[Starting smooth, ending in a growl] Success isn't about waking up at 4 AM, it's about knowing when to say [growing higher pitched] "I need eight hours of sleep to be brilliant!"

[in elderly voice] These old bones have seen thousands graduate, [cough dryly] most forgotten within a semester, [extremely energetic suddenly] but she's rewriting the textbooks I've been teaching from!
[laugh loudly] I journal sometimes! [cry] Mostly lists of people who annoyed me and detailed revenge fantasies involving minor inconveniences. [very quietly] Very therapeutic, actually.
[strong enunciation] You must let me through because [puff] [quickly] I'm absolutely dying to perform at the castle and killing it is my specialty!
[cold] Your mother's grave? [incredulous] That's a bit dramatic for software sales, don't you think? [matter-of-fact] I have to go now, this conversation has become uncomfortable for both of us.

[surprisingly gentle] She won't run. [measured pace] Deep down, Mom knows she needs help—she's probably been waiting for someone to catch her. [long exhale] We'll get through this together, as a family. [voice hardening] But the lying stops tomorrow.
[rapidly muttering] Okay okay focus, target's late, ETA unknown [ahems politely] but this juggler's actually hilarious [speeding up] — wait did he just drop all three balls on purpose?

[grudgingly] Right, right, but we've got an inquiry here folks, [chesty cough], looks like Aunt Martha's calling interference on the juice box incident, [visibly nervous] though I maintain it was within regulation spill parameters.

[flatly] Good for Thompson Industries. [drawn-out syllables] We have our own processes that work just fine. [dismissively] Besides, I heard Thompson's CEO likes to exaggerate his success stories anyway.
[joking whisper] Between you and me, street performer [struggle to breathe] that impression was spot-on [really excited suddenly] — for sure for sure, better than any entertainment at the safe house!
[quaveringly] Her dedication makes me emotional even now... [belch] forgive me, champagne on an empty stomach... [frailty in voice] but I'm too overwhelmed to eat.
[very animated] She's accepting the award right now! [inhale] I need to catch my breath because [growing more trembly with emotion] that brilliant mind once struggled in my classroom.

[anxiously] Em, maybe I should start with the mountain climbing story instead of— [ughh] no, no that's too cliché [words getting lighter and more playful] unless I flip it and talk about choosing NOT to climb every mountain.

[quick jittery bursts] My pulse is racing, thoughts scattered everywhere, [sneeze] this dusty auditorium isn't helping, [sleepily] though after three decades of lectures, I could nap anywhere except today.
The receipt is somewhere in my purse, just give me a second to find it because I know I kept it with all my other papers.

[languidly] Okay, but listen... Emma's executing a beautiful inside maneuver around the bounce house, [hum playfully] maintaining perfect form despite the frosting between her fingers, [no longer quiet] AND SHE'S TAKEN THE LEAD!
[wearily] Yeah yeah, I get paid either way but [whoop] that joke about the banker! [Starting smooth, ending in a growl] Reminds me why I picked this particular profession.
[exasperated] I don't know—therapy maybe? [sharp exhale] Life coaching? Anything where you don't cold call people who clearly aren't interested? [Groans] This is getting genuinely depressing now.

[Theatrically] What a magnificent Tuesday afternoon crowd! [wheeze weakly] Can't breathe from laughing [starting slow, then getting quick] but seriously need to check the rooftop angles, confirm escape routes, mhm mhm, standard protocol.
[flowing speech] You know what's funny about this job — [scoff] — sometimes you're calculating wind trajectories [while laughing] and then boom, a street magician pulls a rabbit from nowhere.

[In a theatric] These gates are truly a-door-able, and you fine gentlemen are the key to my heart! [words getting lighter and more playful] Perhaps you could unlock a little passage for a humble entertainer?
[deadpan] Your employment situation isn't really my concern. [more slowly] I get twelve calls like this every single day. [Sighs heavily] Why should yours be any different from the others?

[Filipino accent, disgusted] There it is—the true face of Spanish mercy. [voice heavy with defeat] Always threats beneath the promises. [quietly determined] I accept your terms, but know this—one day, these islands will be free from you.
[in posh voice] "Maximize your potential" sounds so corporate, so [tsk] predictable [casually] — what if I just tell them about my spectacular failure last Tuesday?
[Croaking] My voice is going [quickly] but eh, vulnerability on stage [chew noisily] might be exactly what connects us all today.

[Filipino accent, steady voice] My family has nothing to hide from tyrants. [pause] [voice cracking slightly] But can you say the same? [whispered urgently] The other encomenderos, they talk about your private dealings with the Dutch merchants.
[Lisping] Will you marry— [oof] damn this retainer! [irate] Why did I schedule dental work this week of all weeks?!

[doubtful expression] Did I really contribute to this moment, or [tone turning mocking halfway through] am I just another professor claiming credit for natural genius? [giggle] But damn, look at her shine!
The website said three to five business days for engraving but I need this by Saturday for her graduation party, so is rush service available?

[flowing speech] And they're off! Little Timmy rounds the coffee table at breakneck speed, [grunt] chocolate cake debris trailing in his wake like a delicious dust cloud, [suddenly dropping in pitch] with Emma closing fast on the inside rail clutching what appears to be... is that Grandma's purse?
My lute isn't a weapon, it has no strings attached! [starting neutral, becoming defensive] Well, technically it does have strings, but [gasp for breath] you know what I mean!
[authoritatively] Listen up, beautiful— [suddenly high energy] NO THAT'S WRONG! [groan] She's not a recruit, you idiot, she's the love of your life!

[bitter laugh] Adorable? [voice cracking] You have no idea what I've become, what I've lost to this curse! [quieter] Every transformation tears me apart—body and soul. Don't patronize me with your centuries of comfortable immortality, parasite.
[in masculine voice] My wife always said I get too attached, but [hum thoughtfully] how can you not when you witness transformation like this, [giggle] it's absolutely intoxicating!
[pensively] So, anyway... we've got a pile-up near the gift table, [strain] three toddlers down in a tangle of wrapping paper, [long pauses after each word] and nobody's crying yet.
I'm thinking maybe the script font would look nicer than the block letters but I'd like to see samples of both before I decide on one.
[overly polite] Excuse me, Senator, if I may just— [cough] sorry about that— [compassionately] the families deserve to know why you voted against the relief fund, don't they?
[exhale] Look, I know you think I'm trying to pull the wool over your eyes, but I'm just a sheep admirer! [suddenly quiet] I swear I'm not trying to fleece anyone here.
[mumbling plead] Don't let this be a dream, please, [visibly nervous] my heart can't take much more of this anticipation but [weep] there's her name engraved in gold forever...
[declaring scornfully] Those recycled quotes about grinding 24/7? [cute laugh] Please, even machines need maintenance [sigh contentedly] — balance is the real power move.
Look, what I mean is [jealous] Anderson Cooper doesn't have to cover pumpkin festivals and spelling bees [monotone] while pretending it's breaking news.
[very excited] Breaking news, folks! [raspy] The hostages are coming out! [relieved] All twelve of them walking on their own power— [melancholic] wait, there should be thirteen.

[Normal voice, frustrated] Ugh, I keep making the accent too subtle. [deep breath] [Filipino accent, bitterly] Progress? [scoff] You mean slavery dressed in pretty words? [voice rising sharply] You destroy our barangays and call it improvement while you steal our gold!
[monotone] I'm sure you do have case studies. [slower pace] Everyone has case studies these days. [Yawns] My calendar is completely booked through next quarter, maybe even longer than that.
I'm not trying to gate-crash your party! [In croaky yell] I have legitimate business! [getting sleepy] Though explaining it is becoming quite exhausting...
